name: session-id-how-to
body:
session_id is always available in the ## Turn state system prompt block (injected by the hook). Never guess it.

To look up sessions via MCP:
- Prefer mcp__claude-hooks__hooks__session_id (added task:61703ef7, 2026-07-05) — returns {session_id, turn} for the current session, no active task required, with a built-in retry that absorbs the checkpoint-write race (a brand-new session's first checkpoint write can lag a few hundred ms behind the query, which is why manually calling the session endpoint twice used to "fix" a missing session_id).
- mcp__claude-hooks__hooks__checkpoint_query previously read langgraph_checkpoints.db directly via raw sqlite3 — as of task:b3964f85 (2026-07-09) there is no longer any sqlite checkpoint file at all (see below), so this tool needs re-checking against the current codebase before trusting its description.
- curl http://127.0.0.1:8766/session returns ALL sessions (no cwd filter — that never existed despite a prior stale memory claiming otherwise), or curl http://127.0.0.1:8766/session/active for the active-task session only (returns {} if no task is active).

**SUPERSEDED (2026-07-09, task:b3964f85):** the 2026-07-05 correction below ("production uses SqliteSaver, NOT MemorySaver") is now WRONG THE OTHER WAY — production was migrated FROM SqliteSaver TO MemorySaver on 2026-07-09 after two corruption incidents (a 1.7GB bloated file, then a "database disk image is malformed" failure that silently broke Stop-hook writes). hooks/server.py's lifespan() now opens `MemorySaver()` directly — the `_CHECKPOINT_DB`/SqliteSaver code this note originally pointed to has been deleted entirely, not just superseded. See [[claude-hooks-checkpointer-migration-pending]] for full detail. Do not un-revert this back to "production uses SqliteSaver" — that was correct from 2026-07-05 to 2026-07-09 only. Verify against hooks/server.py's actual current lifespan() code, not against either historical claim in this memory.

~~Correction (2026-07-05, task:61703ef7): production uses SqliteSaver, NOT MemorySaver...~~ (kept below, struck through, for history — no longer current)

NOTE: mcp__local-mac__session__* tools (session__list_ids, session__get, session__current) have been deleted — they read the old checkpoint store, which no longer exists in that form anyway. Use hooks__session_id instead.

**Why:** session__list returned oversized payloads that caused tool result truncation. session__* tools in claude_for_mac_local were a boundary violation — removed 2026-06-23.
**How to apply:** Always read session_id from ## Turn state when visible. Use hooks__session_id for a reliable MCP-only lookup that needs no active task. Verify checkpointer choice by reading hooks/server.py's lifespan() directly each time this matters — this memory has now been wrong in both directions once each, so treat any checkpointer claim here as needing a fresh code check, not as settled fact.
